title: Feature Importance Audit Snapshot Correlation (Art. 13)

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'EU AI Act' → 'Art.13'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
spl:
index=ml_explain sourcetype="ai:shap:export" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(_time) as last_export by model_version
| join type=left model_version [
    search index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(model_version) as versions_seen by model_version
  ]
| eval stale=if(isnull(last_export) OR (now()-last_export)>2592000,1,0)
| where stale=1
| table model_version, last_export, versions_seen
value: Correlates SHAP export job completion with model_version to retain feature importance audit snapshots for transparency reviews.
implementation: (1) Schedule SHAP export on each release; (2) store artifact_uri in object storage with WORM; (3) alert if model_version in prod without export in 7d; (4) document in technical file.
visualization: Timeline (exports), Table (stale models), Single value (stale count)
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
